hypothesis: Extending CAST to control discrete and graph-structured molecular attributes, the concept bottleneck module can be augmented with a graph neural network encoder operating on molecular scaffolds such that steering vectors derived from scaffold-level representations will enable simultaneous control of both continuous physicochemical properties and categorical structural constraints (e.g., specific ring systems or functional group presence), and this scaffold-conditioned steering will achieve lower property MAE on out-of-distribution targets than property-only steering because scaffold topology provides complementary geometric constraints that reduce the combinatorial search space navigated by the LLM's residual stream during autoregressive SMILES generation.
